title: Button Component
content:
## Button Component This component provides a versatile and customizable button UI element for the DocMed application. It leverages `class-variance-authority` for defining visual variants and sizes, and `@radix-ui/react-slot` for flexible rendering. ### Purpose The `Button` component is designed to be a reusable UI element that adheres to the application's design system. It allows for easy styling based on predefined variants (e.g., `default`, `destructive`, `outline`) and sizes (e.g., `default`, `sm`, `lg`, `icon`). It also supports rendering as a different HTML element via the `asChild` prop. ### Key Exports * **`Button`**: The main React component for rendering buttons. It accepts standard HTML button attributes, along with `variant`, `size`, and `asChild` props. * **`buttonVariants`**: A function from `class-variance-authority` that generates CSS class strings based on the provided `variant` and `size` props. This is primarily used internally by the `Button` component but can be useful for advanced customization or direct class generation. ### Props The `Button` component accepts the following props: * **`variant`** (enum: `'default'`, `'destructive'`, `'outline'`, `'secondary'`, `'ghost'`, `'link'`): Defines the visual style of the button. Defaults to `'default'`. * **`size`** (enum: `'default'`, `'sm'`, `'lg'`, `'icon'`): Defines the size of the button. Defaults to `'default'`. * **`asChild`** (boolean): If `true`, the button will render as the child element provided to it (useful for composing with other components). Defaults to `false`. * **`className`** (string): Allows for adding custom CSS classes to the button. * **`...props`**: Any other standard HTML `button` attributes (e.g., `onClick`, `disabled`, `type`). ### Usage Example ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; function MyComponent() { return (
); } export default MyComponent; ```
